one_line: Tumor serine-metabolism dysregulation lowers sphingomyelin in NK-cell membranes, collapsing membrane protrusions and lyt
phenotype: Loss / shortening of NK-cell membrane protrusions (microvilli); smooth, rounded NK surface
therapeutic_strategy: restore NK sphingomyelin (inhibit SM catabolism: ASM/SMPD1, NSMASE1-3/SMPD2-4) // sphingomyelinase inhibition + Tim3 (HAVCR2) checkpoint blockade

one_line: Tumor-derived adenosine (via CD39/CD73 ectonucleotidases) binds A2A/A2B receptors on NK cells, activating cAMP-PKA signa
phenotype: Suppressed NK cytotoxicity, reduced IFN-gamma secretion, impaired granule polarization
therapeutic_strategy: block adenosine production (anti-CD73 oleclumab / anti-CD39 IPH5201) or block A2AR signaling (CPI-444 / AZD4635) // A2AR antagonist + anti-PD-1/PD-L1 (known clinical synergy) + optionally Tim3 blockad

one_line: Tumor- and CAF-derived TGFβ binds TGFβR on NK cells, suppressing activating receptors (NKG2D, NKp30, NKp44), downregulat
phenotype: Loss of NK activating receptors, reduced cytotoxicity, NK-to-ILC1 conversion, physical NK exclusion
tissue: pan-cancer (gastric, colorectal, pancreatic, glioblastoma, breast, HCC)
therapeutic_strategy: block TGFβ signaling: galunisertib (TGFβR1 inhibitor), fresolimumab (anti-TGFβ pan-isoform), or bintrafusp alfa (TGFβR2-Fc trap fused to anti-PD-L1) // TGFβ blockade + anti-PD-1/PD-L1 (bintrafusp alfa

one_line: Tumor cells shed MICA/B surface ligands via ADAM10/ADAM17 proteolytic cleavage, releasing soluble MICA/B (sMICA/sMICB) t
phenotype: Loss of NKG2D-mediated NK recognition; reduced NK cytotoxicity against MICA/B+ tumors
tissue: pan-cancer (MICA/B overexpressed in many epithelial cancers)
therapeutic_strategy: block ADAM10/17-mediated MICA/B shedding (monoclonal antibody or small-molecule inhibitor) // ADAM10/17 inhibition + anti-Tim3/TIGIT checkpoint blockade
